Title: Regulation of the anthocyanin biosynthetic pathway by the TTG1/bHLH/Myb transcriptional complex in Arabidopsis seedlings

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Anthocyanin branch; shared node with flavonol branch
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Late pathway entry point in Arabidopsis; branch-proximal
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates the B-ring 3' position of flavonoid substrates to generate 3'-hydroxylated anthocyanin/flavonol precursors
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies immediate precursor pool for cyanidin-based anthocyanin production
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; identified as the first late gene under TTG1/bHLH/Myb regulation in seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seedling hypocotyls, cotyledons, root epidermis non-hair files, young emerging leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression is positively regulated by the TTG1/bHLH/Myb complex and supports anthocyanin accumulation; downregulated in Myb RNAi, ttg1, and bHLH mutants
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript reduced in myb RNAi, ttg1, and strong bHLH mutants; induced in GL3:GR and TTG1:GR assays
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Endogenous promoter assayed by qPCR; indirectly also under control of 35S-driven GL3:GR and TTG1:GR regulators
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to enhance anthocyanin flux when co-activated with late-pathway regulators; likely best deployed with DFR/LDOX and regulatory genes rather than alone
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May alter flux partitioning between flavonol and anthocyanin branches
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper concludes Arabidopsis late anthocyanin regulation begins at F3'H; discussion suggests F3'H may also be partly controlled by TTG1-independent mechanisms
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. F3'H is downregulated in Myb knockdown, ttg1, and bHLH mutant seedlings. 2. GL3:GR induction upregulates F3'H, including under DEX+CHX, supporting direct regulation by GL3. 3. TTG1:GR also upregulates F3'H, but CHX tests suggest it is a secondary TTG1 target. 4. The study places F3'H at the start of the late anthocyanin gene set regulated by the TTG1/bHLH/Myb complex.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 3% sucrose; continuous white light; DEX induction; DEX+CHX treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR in myb RNAi and regulatory mutants; GL3:GR and TTG1:GR induction with CHX test; correlation with anthocyanin phenotypes

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms direct precursor to anthocyanidin synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key late structural gene under TTG1/bHLH/Myb control
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seedling hypocotyls, cotyledons, root epidermis, young leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive target of TTG1-dependent Myb and bHLH regulators; reduced expression correlates with anthocyanin deficiency
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated in Myb RNAi, ttg1 and bHLH mutants; induced by GL3:GR and TTG1:GR
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong engineering target for boosting anthocyanin output when activated with upstream hydroxylation and downstream oxidation/glycosylation steps
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May redirect flavonoid flux away from alternative branches
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as a canonical late gene in Arabidopsis flavonoid regulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DFR is consistently downregulated in Myb RNAi, ttg1, and bHLH mutant seedlings. 2. GL3:GR and TTG1:GR activate DFR even with CHX, indicating direct regulation by the TTG1-dependent complex. 3. Reduced DFR expression tracks anthocyanin-deficient phenotypes.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 3% sucrose; continuous white light; DEX induction; DEX+CHX treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR in mutants and inducible GR assays

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms immediate anthocyanidin precursor to stable anthocyanins
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key late structural gene under TTG1/bHLH/Myb control
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seedling hypocotyls, cotyledons, root epidermis, young leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activated by TTG1-dependent regulators; reduced expression associates with anthocyanin loss
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated in Myb RNAi, ttg1, and bHLH mutants; induced by GL3:GR and TTG1:GR
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important stacked engineering target with DFR and glycosylation/transport genes for stable anthocyanin enhancement
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Differential regulation analysis suggested EGL3 contributes more than GL3 to native LDOX activation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LDOX is a late anthocyanin structural gene downregulated in Myb RNAi, ttg1, and bHLH mutants. 2. GL3:GR and TTG1:GR activate LDOX in DEX+CHX assays, supporting direct regulation. 3. Expression data suggest EGL3 has a stronger role than GL3 for full activation in seedlings.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 3% sucrose; continuous white light; DEX induction; DEX+CHX treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR in mutants and inducible GR assays

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers sugar moieties to anthocyanin pathway products, contributing to pigment stability/intensity
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Coordinately downregulated with late anthocyanin genes in Myb, bHLH, and ttg1 mutants, supporting positive regulation by the TTG1-dependent complex
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reduced in Myb RNAi, bHLH mutants, and ttg1 seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate to improve anthocyanin stability and color intensity when stacked with biosynthetic and transport genes
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could alter anthocyanin decoration patterns and color tone
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because study specifically tested and concluded coordinate late-pathway regulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT75C1 expression is reduced in Myb RNAi, bHLH, and ttg1 mutants. 2. The study identifies UGT75C1 as part of the late anthocyanin gene set coordinately regulated by the TTG1-dependent complex. 3. It was examined because anthocyanin glycosylation contributes to pigment intensity and color.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Anthocyanin biosynthetic pathway / anthocyanin sequestration-transport module
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acts as an anthocyanin-associated glutathione S-transferase required for anthocyanin accumulation/sequestration rather than core synthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively associated with anthocyanin accumulation; expression is reduced in Myb RNAi, bHLH, and ttg1 mutants
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reduced in Myb RNAi, bHLH mutants, and ttg1 seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for improving anthocyanin accumulation/stability in combination with biosynthetic activators
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May influence vacuolar trafficking of other phenolics
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although not a catalytic pathway enzyme for anthocyanin synthesis, the paper treats it as a late anthocyanin structural/pathway-associated gene
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GST12 is downregulated in Myb RNAi, bHLH, and ttg1 seedlings. 2. The gene is coordinately regulated with late anthocyanin biosynthetic genes by the TTG1-dependent complex. 3. Its inclusion broadens the regulated late gene set beyond core enzymatic steps.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Hypocotyls and cotyledons of young seedlings; young emerging leaves
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation as part of TTG1/bHLH/MYB complex
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin biosynthesis via activation of late flavonoid genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates late anthocyanin genes; little to no effect on early genes PAL1 and CHS in native loss-of-function context
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Predominant Myb required for anthocyanin accumulation in seedlings
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of PAP1 alone causes anthocyanin-deficient seedlings, while PAP1 overactivity is known to strongly increase pigment
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAP1 is the most strongly expressed of the four PAP-related MYBs in young seedlings. 2. PAP1-specific RNAi and a pap1 insertion mutant both show reduced anthocyanin accumulation, showing PAP1 alone is sufficient for normal seedling pigmentation. 3. The study supports PAP1 as a regulator of late anthocyanin genes rather than broad early phenylpropanoid genes.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Developmental stage; sucrose-inducing conditions discussed; requires TTG1 and bHLH partners for output
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions in TTG1/bHLH/MYB transcriptional complex with TTG1 and GL3/EGL3/TT8-class bHLHs
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly expressed in young seedlings; high in hypocotyls and cotyledons at 2-3 days, then fades and becomes restricted to young emerging leaves; not expressed in root
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Same as source species; predominant among PAP-related MYBs in seedlings
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: PAP1-specific RNAi; pap1 insertion mutant; RT-PCR; promoter:GUS expression; mutant phenotype analysis
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: PAP1 endogenous promoter in GUS reporter; PAP1-specific RNAi transgene driven by constitutive promoter
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper argues PAP1 does not regulate the proanthocyanidin branch in seed coat
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: AtGenExpress database expression patterns cited; no accession provided

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Late anthocyanin genes; exact direct targets not resolved here
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Positive regulator by family assignment; PAP2 transcript is a secondary target of GL3 induction
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Contributes redundantly but is not the predominant seedling MYB
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Part of the redundant PAP-related MYB set promoting anthocyanin accumulation, but PAP2-specific silencing alone shows little visible seedling effect
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAP2 is one of four related MYBs targeted in the multi-Myb RNAi line that causes anthocyanin deficiency. 2. PAP2 transcript is barely detectable in seedlings and PAP2-specific RNAi has little effect on seedling anthocyanin. 3. GL3:GR induction increases PAP2 expression only in the absence of CHX, indicating PAP2 is a secondary target of GL3.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Barely detectable in seedlings by RT-PCR; little/no GUS detected in seedling promoter lines
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 3% sucrose; continuous white light; DEX induction
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Multi-Myb RNAi; PAP2-specific RNAi; RT-PCR; GL3:GR induction assay
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S:GL3:GR used to test upstream induction; PAP2 endogenous promoter:GUS
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a major determinant of seedling anthocyanin under tested conditions

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Pigmented embryos, cotyledons, hypocotyls, root epidermal non-hair files, young leaves
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation as part of TTG1/bHLH/MYB complex
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Late anthocyanin genes including F3'H; DFR; LDOX; GST12; UGT75C1 inferred through RNAi family loss
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes expression of late anthocyanin genes; requires TTG1 and bHLH proteins for effective output
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Strong positive activator when overexpressed, but dependent on TTG1 and bHLH context
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: 35S:Myb113 causes strong anthocyanin overaccumulation, while combined knockdown with related MYBs causes anthocyanin deficiency
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Overexpression of Myb113 strongly elevates anthocyanin accumulation in embryos and seedlings. 2. Pigment induction by Myb113 requires TTG1 and bHLH proteins, as activity is reduced in egl3, gl3 egl3, ttg1 and bHLH triple mutant backgrounds. 3. Multi-Myb RNAi lines that include Myb113 show reduced expression of late anthocyanin genes and strong anthocyanin deficiency.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: TTG1 and bHLH partner availability; developmental expression domains of GL3/EGL3
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Low transcript abundance in seedlings; little/no promoter:GUS signal in seedlings
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpression induces pigment where bHLH partners are present
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 35S overexpression; genetic interaction with egl3, gl3 egl3, ttg1 and bHLH mutant backgrounds; multi-Myb RNAi; RT-PCR; anthocyanin phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Pigment pattern under overexpression mirrors GL3/EGL3 expression domains, emphasizing partner dependence

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Pigmented embryos, seedlings, early rosette leaves, trichome-associated leaf regions
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation as part of TTG1/bHLH/MYB complex
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Late anthocyanin genes inferred through family function
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes anthocyanin production in a bHLH-dependent pattern
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive activator when full-length Ler allele is overexpressed; natural Col allele is truncated
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Ler Myb114 overexpression induces strong anthocyanin accumulation, whereas the truncated Col allele behaves as a dominant repressor of TTG1-dependent epidermal pathways
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The Ler allele encodes a full-length anthocyanin-promoting MYB, while the Col allele contains a premature stop codon after the MYB domain. 2. 35S:Myb114L produces strong pigment accumulation in seedlings and leaves in patterns overlapping bHLH expression. 3. Pigment induction is bHLH-dependent. 4. Overexpression of truncated Col Myb114 causes dominant repression of TTG1-dependent epidermal traits including anthocyanin deficiency.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: TTG1 and bHLH partner availability; natural allelic variation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Low in seedlings by RT-PCR; little/no promoter:GUS signal in seedlings
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpression leads to anthocyanin in rosette leaf regions corresponding to bHLH expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Allele comparison; 35S overexpression of Ler and Col alleles; anthocyanin phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Dominant-negative truncated alleles can repress TTG1-dependent epidermal cell fate pathways including trichome development
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Important natural allelic functional difference between Col and Ler accessions

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seedling cotyledons, hypocotyl, root epidermal non-hair cells, young leaves where regulator is expressed
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly activates F3'H, DFR, LDOX and TT8; PAP2 is a secondary target
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential positive regulator, but with function partially overlapping and distinct from EGL3
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for full anthocyanin accumulation in tissues where it is expressed; differential target activation relative to EGL3 shapes pigment pattern
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GL3 expression overlaps anthocyanin accumulation domains in seedlings. 2. Inducible GL3:GR directly activates late anthocyanin structural genes and TT8. 3. Genetic comparisons show GL3 and EGL3 are functionally diversified, with GL3 contributing less than EGL3 to DFR/LDOX activation.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Developmental expression pattern; DEX in GL3:GR system
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Activates TT8, creating regulatory reinforcement within the complex network
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Part of TTG1/bHLH/MYB complex with TTG1 and PAP MYBs
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: TTG1; PAP1; PAP2; Myb113; Myb114; EGL3; TT8
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: In young seedlings, expressed in epidermal files and organs matching pigment domains; later refined to specific growing tissues
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Same as source species; overexpressed via 35S:GL3:GR in inducible assays
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 3% sucrose; continuous white light; DEX induction; DEX+CHX treatment
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter:GUS; mutant analysis; GL3:GR rescue and induction assays; qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: ProGL3:GUS endogenous promoter reporter; CaMV 35S for GL3:GR
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: More limited contribution than EGL3 to some targets despite strong overexpression

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Hypocotyls and other seedling tissues where expressed
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: TT8; F3'H; DFR; LDOX inferred from mutant comparisons
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Makes strong positive contribution to activation of late anthocyanin genes, especially TT8, DFR and LDOX
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major positive regulator with stronger apparent role than GL3 for several anthocyanin targets
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of EGL3 sharply reduces anthocyanin in tissues where EGL3 is normally expressed and reduces Myb113-driven pigment output
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EGL3 expression overlaps anthocyanin accumulation in seedlings. 2. Genetic comparisons indicate EGL3 contributes more strongly than GL3 to activation of TT8, DFR and LDOX. 3. Loss of EGL3 diminishes anthocyanin production and alters response to Myb113 overexpression.
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: TTG1; PAP1; PAP2; Myb113; Myb114; GL3; TT8
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in young seedlings in patterns overlapping pigment accumulation; refined with development
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter:GUS; egl3 mutant and gl3 egl3 double mutant analysis; interaction with Myb113 overexpression; qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional diversification versus GL3 is a major conclusion of the study

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Activation of late anthocyanin pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly upregulates DFR, LDOX and TT8; F3'H behaves as a secondary TTG1 target
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential co-regulator required for TTG1-dependent anthocyanin gene expression and pigment accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of TTG1 causes strong anthocyanin deficiency and blocks output from overexpressed Myb113
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TTG1 is required for anthocyanin accumulation driven by PAP-related MYBs. 2. ttg1 mutants downregulate late genes F3'H, DFR, LDOX, GST12 and UGT75C1 but not early genes. 3. TTG1:GR induction directly activates DFR, LDOX and TT8, while F3'H is likely indirect.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: DEX in TTG1:GR assays; availability of bHLH and MYB partners
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Promotes TT8 expression, reinforcing activity of the TTG1-dependent complex
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Core component of TTG1/bHLH/MYB transcriptional complex
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GL3; EGL3; TT8; PAP1; PAP2; Myb113; Myb114
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Assayed through 35S:TTG1:GR induction in ttg1 seedlings
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 3% sucrose; continuous white light; DEX induction; DEX+CHX treatment
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ttg1 mutant analysis; TTG1:GR rescue and induction assays; qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TTG1 dependence distinguishes the late anthocyanin regulatory module from other flavonoid regulators

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation within TTG1/bHLH/MYB complex network
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Acts positively in anthocyanin regulation; itself directly activated by GL3 and TTG1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Contributes to TTG1-dependent bHLH activity; part of the severe anthocyanin-deficient triple/quadruple bHLH mutant backgrounds
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss contributes to reduced anthocyanin accumulation; transcript is directly induced by GL3 and TTG1
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TT8 is part of the TTG1-dependent bHLH set required for anthocyanin production. 2. GL3:GR and TTG1:GR directly upregulate TT8. 3. Differential regulation experiments suggest EGL3 contributes strongly to TT8 expression.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Positive feedforward reinforcement within the TTG1-dependent regulatory module
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions as a bHLH component of the TTG1-dependent complex
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reduced in strong bHLH mutant backgrounds; induced by GL3:GR and TTG1:GR
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 3% sucrose; continuous white light; DEX induction; DEX+CHX treatment
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; GL3:GR and TTG1:GR qRT-PCR induction assays
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Treated as both a regulator and a direct target within the network
